key_insight: A single attractive crowder species with saturable binding is enough to drive a full coil-globule-coil reentrant transition in polymers as crowder volume fraction increases.
engineering_relevance: This identifies crowder density and binding saturation as a minimal control knob for tuning polymer compaction, which is relevant to designing crowded soft-matter, biomolecular, and charged-polymer systems.
method: Molecular dynamics simulations of coarse-grained polymers with attractive crowders
Scan crowder volume fraction to observe collapse followed by reexpansion
Compare against purely repulsive crowders and charged-polymer cases with explicit counterions
evidence_signal: The abstract reports simulated conformational behavior and size-distribution trends showing reentrant transition with attractive crowders, monotonic compaction with repulsive crowders, and altered statistics that do not collapse onto a universal SAW curve.
watch_item: The mechanism depends on saturable monomer-crowder binding, so results may be sensitive to model details such as interaction strengths, crowder valency, and coarse-graining assumptions.
paper_title: Density-driven reentrant polymer transitions via saturable bridging crowders
